text: The surgeon uses a robotic needle driver to perform the reconstruction of the inner layer of the resection bed. A 3-0 monocryl suture on an SH needle is passed through the renal capsule into the defect, with each suture anchored by a knot and a hemlock clip on the renal capsule.

The surgeon continues suturing the resection bed in a continuous manner, closing small vessels and repairing any entries into the collecting system. Care is taken to avoid placing sutures too deeply to prevent vascular injury.

The surgeon passes the needle from the inner layer to the outside of the kidney through the renal capsule, securing the stitch with a hemlock clip placed by the assistant. Additional sutures are applied to occlude small bleeding vessels, contributing to hemostasis during renoraphy.
